instinct_candidates.title: Keep compound instincts docs + JSON in lockstep
instinct_candidates.trigger: When adding/removing/editing a compound instinct (or changing its id/title/action)
instinct_candidates.action: Update both `.loom/compound/INSTINCTS.md` (human-readable catalog) and `.loom/compound/instincts.json` (compiled/consumed form) in the same change; ensure ids are kebab-case, stable, and referenced consistently across skills/episodes; avoid near-duplicates by merging semantics into one instinct when possible.

instinct_candidates.title: Treat .opencode/skills as the canonical skill location
instinct_candidates.trigger: When reading, updating, or proposing skills for Loom / OpenCode
instinct_candidates.action: Prefer `.opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` as the source-of-truth; if a parallel legacy path exists (e.g. `.claude/skills/...`), do not create/maintain duplicates—converge on the `.opencode/skills` copy and scrub references to legacy locations in guidance.

instinct_candidates.title: Use vendor-neutral wording in compound guidance
instinct_candidates.trigger: When writing skills/prompts/docs that will be reused across environments or toolchains
instinct_candidates.action: Avoid vendor/product-specific language and paths (e.g. naming a specific assistant or referencing `.claude/...`); write instructions in tool-agnostic terms that map to Loom/OpenCode primitives (`.opencode/skills`, `.loom/compound/...`, instincts/episodes) so guidance survives migrations.

skill_candidates.name: compound-autolearn-proposal
skill_candidates.description: Generate durable, memory-only instinct/skill proposals from session context; output strict JSON only and do nothing when learning is not durable.
skill_candidates.body:
# Compound Autolearn Proposal (Memory-Only)

Purpose: propose durable memory updates (instincts/skills) from the provided session context. Do not write product code.

## Hard Rules
- Do not run tools.
- Do not propose product code changes.
- Output a single JSON object only; if nothing durable, output `{}`.
- Prefer updating an existing skill over creating near-duplicates.
- Keep within caps: max 3 skills, max 8 instincts.

## Inputs You Can Use
- `AUTOLEARN CONTEXT` block (git summary + recent observations).
- Any explicit file paths mentioned.

## Procedure
1) Scan the git summary for what actually changed.
- If changes are mostly churn (formatting, renames, one-off experiment), bias toward `{}`.
- If changes show repeated patterns (new instincts added, duplicated skill paths, repeated grep for certain terms), consider an instinct.

2) Extract 1-3 durable learnings.
- Durable = likely to recur across sessions and materially improves quality/consistency.
- Not durable = task-specific decision, one-off fix, or anything depending on hidden context.

3) Propose instinct candidates.
- Use stable kebab-case ids.
- Write triggers that are easy to recognize ("when adding an instinct", "when proposing skills").
- Actions should be specific and operational (what to do, where).

4) Propose skill updates only if needed.
- Only create/replace a skill when the procedure itself is missing or drifting.
- Keep skills short and procedural.
- Include full body (this file) as the managed content.

## Quality Checklist (before output)
- Does each proposal reduce future confusion or drift?
- Does it avoid duplicating an existing concept?
- Are paths repo-root-relative (e.g. `.opencode/skills/...`, `.loom/compound/...`)?
- Are confidence scores honest (0.5 = weak signal; 0.8+ = strong evidence)?

## Output Format
Return a JSON object with optional keys:
- `instinct_candidates`: list of `{ id, title, trigger, action, confidence, tags }`
- `skill_candidates`: list of `{ name, description, body, tags, source_instinct_ids }`

If nothing durable: `{}`
